title: Taraxacum geirhildae

taxonomy: Taraxacum geirhildae is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family Asteraceae. It is a type of dandelion that is endemic to Iceland. The classification of Taraxacum geirhildae includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, order as Asterales, family as Asteraceae, genus as Taraxacum, and species as T. geirhildae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic analysis. Taraxacum geirhildae is often placed in the same section as the common dandelion, Taraxacum officinale.
benefits: Taraxacum geirhildae is a lesser-known species of dandelion, and research on its health benefits is limited. However, its close relative, Taraxacum officinale, is known for its potential health benefits. Taraxacum officinale may help to support digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome due to its prebiotic properties. constituents: Taraxacum geirhildae is a species of flowering plant, however, I couldn't find specific information about its active constituents.
